rationale: Review 2's main generality findings rest on debatable premises: the ℕ-indexed induction is the natural proof engine (not a 'less general' choice), and the Fintype/Fin corollaries are standard derived results. Its remaining findings (∃ a b : F, parameter order) are contested stylistic preferences. Review 1 correctly approves; acting on either review leaves the PR essentially equally good on generality.
